ref: But Cain had to go far from the Lord and live in the Land of Wandering, which is east of Eden. More and More People
text: Kain da yi Zugsɔb la san'an keŋ bɛ Nod teŋin, Eden nya'aŋ sa.
translated: Now the Lord God had planted a garden in the east, in Eden.

ref: Lamech and Zillah had a son named Tubal Cain who made tools out of bronze and iron. They also had a daughter, whose name was Naamah.
text: Ka Lamek pu'abil Zilla mɛ da du'a biig, ka o yʋ'ʋr buon ye Tuebal Kain. Ka onɛ mɛ da pin'il kʋnt tʋʋma wʋsa, ka o taʋn yʋ'ʋr da buon Naama.
translated: And Lamech’s wife Zillah also gave birth to a son, and she named him Tubal-cain. He became a forger of all kinds of bronze and iron tools, and the sister of Tubal-cain was Naamah.

ref: Anyone who tries to get even with me will be punished ten times more than anyone who tries to get even with Cain.”
text: Ba ya'a kʋ nidiba ayɔpɔi yɔ Kain kʋʋb sam, ba na kʋ nidib pisyɔpɔi nɛ ayɔpɔi yɔ man Lamek kʋʋb sam!”
translated: If Cain is avenged seven times, then Lamech seventy-seven times!

ref: Adam and his wife had another son. They named him Seth, because they said, “God has given us a son to take the place of Abel, who was killed by his brother Cain.”
text: Ka Adam nɛ Hawa da lɛn du'a biig, ka Hawa da pʋd o yʋ'ʋr ye Set, ka yɛl ye, “Wina'am tisim biig nwa' ka o lɛdig Abɛl zin'igin, Kain n kʋ Abɛl la zug.”
translated: When Adam and Eve had another child, Eve gave birth to a son named Seth, and said, "God has given me another child to replace Abel, because Cain killed Abel."

text: ka pʋd o yʋ'ʋr ye Noa, ka yɛl ye, “Bikaŋa na bʋgʋli ti sʋʋnr nɛ namisʋg kanɛ ka ti nyɛt kuobin nɛ ti nu'us tʋʋma wʋsa ni, ka li yinɛ Wina'am da pɔ nɔɔri niŋ teŋ la.”
translated: Then the Lord said to Noah, “Come out of the ark, you and your wife and your sons and their wives with you, and bring out every kind of living creature that is with you.”

text: ) la, o da du'a Sem nɛ Ham nɛ Jafet.
translated: And then, he begot Shem, Ham, and Japheth.

ref: The children of the supernatural beings who had married these women became famous heroes and warriors. They were called Nephilim and lived on the earth at that time and even later.
text: Ka Wina'am biis la da di pu'a vɛnla la ka digin nɛ ba la, ba da du'a bibanɛ da lieb gandaad ka ba da buonnɛ ba ye Nefil dim. Ba da anɛ nimbɛda banɛ mɔr paŋ, ka ba bɛ saŋkan la nɛ li nya'aŋ.
translated: When the sons of God went to the daughters of men and had children by them, they became the heroes of old, men of renown. The LORD saw how great the wickedness of the human race had become on the earth, and that every inclination of the thoughts of the human heart was only evil all the time.

ref: I'm going to send a flood that will destroy everything that breathes! Nothing will be left alive.
text: M na kɛ ka saa ni ka ku'om kena pɛ'ɛl dunia n di ninsaalib, nɛ linɛ wʋsa mɔr vʋm teŋgbauŋ la zug.
translated: If I say that death is the end of life, then I am only repeating what everyone already knows.

ref: Not a bird, animal, reptile, or human was left alive anywhere on earth.
text: Bʋnvʋya banɛ da bɛ teŋgbauŋ zug la wʋsa da kpiya. Niis nɛ yin bʋnkɔnbid nɛ sian'arin bʋnkɔnbid nɛ banɛ wʋsa vuud teŋ nɛ saalib wʋsa.
translated: The hunter went to the bush and killed an antelope. He brought both the front leg and the back leg of the antelope home and gave them to his wife to cook.

ref: A hundred fifty days later, the water started going down.
text: Ka ku'om la da pɛ'ɛl teŋgbauŋ la dabisa kɔbiga nɛ pisnu.
translated: If you want to learn quickly, listen to the elders and be patient.

ref: The water kept going down, and the mountain tops could be seen on the first day of the tenth month.
text: Ku'om la da maligim siednɛ hali, ka nwadis piiga la pin'ilʋg daar ka zuoya zut yʋ'ʋn nied.
translated: The chief sat in his palace, and the elders brought a goat and poured libation before him.

ref: “You, your wife, your sons, and your daughters-in-law may now leave the boat.
text: “Yim anrʋŋʋn la, fʋn nɛ fʋ pu'a nɛ fʋ biis nɛ ba pu'ab la.
translated: If you are well, your father and your mother and your children are also well.

ref: the living creatures left in groups of their own kind. The Lord's Promise for the Earth
text: Bʋnkɔnbid la wʋsa nɛ banɛ wʋsa vuud teŋin nɛ niis nɛ linɛ wʋsa bɛ teŋgbauŋʋn la da yi anrʋŋʋn la fuor-fuor.
translated: The chameleon changes its color and its body so that people and animals cannot easily see it.

ref: The smell of the burning offering pleased the Lord, and he said: Never again will I punish the earth for the sinful things its people do. All of them have evil thoughts from the time they are young, but I will never destroy everything that breathes, as I did this time.
text: Ka Zugsɔb la wʋmmi li nyuug ka li malis o, ka o yɛl o sʋʋnrin ye, “M kʋ lɛm pɔ nɔɔri tis teŋ la ninsaal zugɔ, la'an nɛ ninsaal wʋsa pʋtɛn'ɛre an bɛ'ɛd lin yi o biilimin sa laa. M mɛ kʋ lɛn san'am bʋnvʋya nwɛnɛ mam daa maal si'em laa.
translated: And the elder took the child and held him, and he said to his mother, “I have seen many children come into this world, but I have never seen a child as beautiful and bright as this one. I pray that he will grow up to be a blessing to his family and to all of us.”

ref: God said to Noah and his sons: I am giving you my blessing. Have a lot of children and grandchildren, so people will live everywhere on this earth.
text: Ka Wina'am niŋ Noa nɛ o biis la bareka ka yɛli ba ye, “Du'ami ka nɔbigi pɛ'ɛli teŋgbauŋ la.
translated: When Wina'am and Noah and his children left the boat, they said, "Let us build a house so that we will not be scattered everywhere."

ref: But life is in the blood, and you must not eat any meat that still has blood in it.
text: Amaa mid ka ya ɔnbi ni'im kanɛ ka li ziim nan kpɛm bɛɛ li ni.
translated: But if you do not have faith, you will not stand firm.

ref: I want you and your descendants to have many children, so people will live everywhere on earth.
text: Amaa ya'a anɛ fʋn Noa nɛ fʋ biis la, du'ami ka nɔbigi pɛ'ɛli dunia la.”
translated: But just as it was in Noah’s days, so it will be when the children of men fill the earth.

ref: The rainbow will be the sign of that solemn promise. Noah and His Family
text: Ka Wina'am lɛn yɛl Noa ye, “Nɛ'ɛŋa sid an m nɔnaar la zanbin, linɛ bɛ man nɛ dunia bʋnvʋr wʋsa sʋʋgin.”
translated: Then God said to Noah, “I have decided to put an end to all people, for the earth is full of their violence.”

ref: Arpachshad had more children and died at the age of
text: Sela du'am nya'aŋ la Arfaksad da lɛn du'a biribis nɛ bipumis sieba ka vʋe paae yʋma kɔbisnaasi nɛ atan' (
translated: After the flood, Arphaxad was born two years later and he had many descendants and lived for many years and died.

ref: Terah decided to move from Ur to the land of Canaan. He took along Abram and Sarai and his grandson Lot, the son of Haran. But when they came to the city of Haran, they settled there instead.
text: Tera da nɔk o biig Abram nɛ o yaaŋ Lot onɛ an Aran biig la, nɛ o bipu'a Sarai onɛ an o biig Abram pu'a la, ka ba yi Ur linɛ bɛ Kaldea teŋin la, ye ba keŋ Keenan teŋin. Ka ban da paae Haran tempʋʋgin la ba da zin'in anina.
translated: Terah took his son Abram and his grandson Lot, who was the son of Haran, and his daughter-in-law Sarai, the wife of his son Abram, and they set out from Ur of the Chaldeans to go to the land of Canaan. But when they came to Haran, they settled there.

ref: As soon as Abram and Sarai arrived in Egypt, the Egyptians noticed how beautiful she was.
text: Abram da paae Egipt la, nidib la sid nyɛɛ o pu'a la ka o vɛnl hali.
translated: Abram went down to Egypt, because the famine in the land was severe.

ref: The king was good to Abram because of Sarai, and Abram was given sheep, cattle, donkeys, slaves, and camels.
text: Ka Sarai zug, na'ab Faaro da maal Abram sʋ'ʋŋa ka Abram paam pɛ'ɛs nɛ niigi nɛ bʋŋdaad nɛ bʋŋnya'as, nɛ yamdaad nɛ yampu'ab nɛ yʋgʋma.
translated: After these things, King Pharaoh took all Abram's possessions and Abram became very rich in livestock, in silver and in gold, in servants and in maidservants and in camels.

ref: Because of Sarai, the Lord struck the king and everyone in his palace with terrible diseases.
text: Ka Zugsɔb kɛ ka ban'abɛ'og kpɛn' Na'ab Faaro yin Abram pu'a Sarai yɛla.
translated: When Zugsɔb and his people met Chief Pharaoh, he took Abram's wife Sarai.

ref: So the king told his men to let Abram and Sarai take their possessions and leave.
text: Ka Na'ab Faaro tis o nidib la nɔɔr ye ba yisim o nɛ o pu'a la nɛ on mɔr si'el wʋsa ka ba gaad.
translated: When King Pharaoh saw his dream, he called his wise men and magicians to explain it but they could not.

ref: Abram moved from place to place in the Southern Desert. And finally, he went north and set up his tents between Bethel and Ai,
text: Ka ba yi Negef n keŋ zin'is kɔn'ɔb-kɔn'ɔb hali ti paae Betel, zin'ikanɛ bɛ Betel nɛ Ai teŋsʋk
translated: He went toward the Negev and pitched his tent, with Bethel on the west and Ai on the east.

ref: where he had earlier camped and built an altar. There he worshiped the Lord.
text: ka o da dɛŋi mɛ' maan bimbim la. Ani ka Abram da lɛm buol Zugsɔb la yʋ'ʋri wɔ'ɔg o.
translated: And he believed what was said to him. And Abram believed the LORD, and he counted it to him as righteousness.

ref: Lot, who was traveling with him, also had sheep, goats, and cattle, as well as his own family and slaves.
text: Lot onɛ da dɔl Abram la mɛ da mɔr pɛ'ɛs nɛ niigi nɛ sʋga.
translated: Lot, who went with Abram, also had flocks, herds, and tents.

ref: This happened before the Lord had destroyed the cities of Sodom and Gomorrah. And when Lot looked around, he saw there was plenty of water in the Jordan Valley. All the way to Zoar the valley was as green as the garden of the Lord or the land of Egypt.
text: Lot da gɔsi kpa' Joodan kɔldaug bɔn'ɔg la nyɛ zin'ikanɛ sɔbig ka ma'ae nwɛnɛ Zugsɔb lɔmbɔn'ɔg la, bɛɛ nwɛnɛ Egipt teŋ linɛ kpa' Zoar tempʋʋg la. Saŋkan la ka Zugsɔb nam pʋ san'am Sodom nɛ Gomora tɛɛns la.
translated: Lot and his daughters left Jordan valley and settled in the mountains because he was afraid to stay in Zoar. He and his two daughters lived in a cave. This was after God destroyed Sodom and Gomorrah.

ref: I will give you and your family all the land you can see. It will be theirs forever!
text: Fʋ nini nyɛ paae teŋ si'a la wʋsa mam na nɔki tisif nɛ fʋ yaas saŋa wʋsa.
translated: If you want good things in life, you must work hard and not just sit and wait for them.

ref: I will give you more descendants than there are specks of dust on the earth, and someday it will be easier to count those specks of dust than to count your descendants.
text: M na kɛ ka fʋ yaas la nwɛnɛ titanbin'isʋg nɛ, ka sɔ' kae na nyaŋi kaali ba.
translated: If you don't put your things in their proper places, it will be hard to find them later.

ref: attacked King Bera of Sodom, King Birsha of Gomorrah, King Shinab of Admah, King Shemeber of Zeboiim, and the king of Bela, a city also known as Zoar.
text: Ka ba da yi tanpi tʋ'ʋs Bera, onɛ da an Sodom na'ab, nɛ Birsea onɛ da an Gomora na'ab, nɛ Sinab onɛ da an Adma na'ab, nɛ Semebɛr onɛ da an Zeboim na'ab, nɛ Bela teŋ na'ab.
translated: These were the kings: Bera, king of Sodom; Birsha, king of Gomorrah; Shinab, king of Admah; Shemeber, king of Zeboiim; and the king of Bela (that is, Zoar).

ref: Abram returned after he had defeated King Chedorlaomer and the other kings. Then the king of Sodom went to meet Abram in Shaveh Valley, which is also known as King's Valley.
text: Abram n da zabi nyaŋ Kedolma na'ab nɛ na'abanɛ wʋsa da la'as nɛ o ka lɛbid na la, Sodom Na'ab da yina sia'al o Seave Bɔn'ɔg zin'igin.
translated: Abram heard that his relative had been taken captive, so he led out his trained men, born in his house, and went in pursuit as far as Dan. The king of Sodom also went out to meet him in the Valley of Shaveh, that is, the King's Valley.

ref: and said to Abram: “I bless you in the name of God Most High, Creator of heaven and earth.
text: Ka o niŋ Abram bareka yɛt ye, “Wina'am Onɛ Gaad Si'el Wʋsa, ka maal agɔl nɛ teŋ la niŋim Abram zug albareka.
translated: When Abram was ninety-nine years old, the LORD appeared to Abram and said to him, “I am God Almighty; walk before me and be blameless.

ref: Let my share be the food that my men have eaten. But Aner, Eshcol, and Mamre went with me, so give them their share of what we brought back.
text: Ayei, mam kʋ di'e si'ela, ya'a ka' dikanɛ ka m nidib la di laa. Amaa kɛl ka Anɛr nɛ Esikol nɛ Mamir banɛ dɔllimi keŋ zaba la paami ba pʋʋr.”
translated: Well, I don't know about that, but I heard that Anɛr, Esikol, and Mamir all went to the farm together yesterday.

ref: As the sun was setting, Abram fell into a deep sleep, and everything became dark and frightening.
text: Ka winnigi da lut la, gbɛɛm da zɛɛg Abram ka o gbis ka zin'ig la lieb lik biridi ka dabiem kpɛn' o.
translated: When the sun had set, darkness fell and Abram saw a smoking firepot with a blazing torch appear and pass between the pieces.

ref: Four generations later, your descendants will return here and take this land, because only then will the people who live here be so sinful that they deserve to be punished.
text: Zʋmaaŋ kanɛ paas anaasi la ni, fʋ biis na ti lɛb kpɛla na, bɔzugɔ Amor dim tʋʋmbɛ'ɛd la nam pʋ paae li bɛnnɛ.”
translated: If you want to get married, you must first pay the bride price, otherwise the elders will not allow you to take the woman.

ref: Sometime after sunset, when it was very dark, a smoking cooking pot and a flaming fire passed between the two halves of each animal.
text: Ka winnigi da li ka yʋ'ʋŋ sɔb la, Abram nyɛ ka bugum nɛ nyɔ'ɔs dɔl bʋnkɔnbbu'ama la teŋsʋki gaad.
translated: When it was dark and night had fallen, Abram saw a smoking firepot and a blazing torch pass between the pieces.

ref: They will possess the land of the Kenites, the Kenizzites, the Kadmonites,
text: Ken dim sʋ'ʋlim la, nɛ Kenas dim nɛ Kadmon dim
translated: Ken begot Sʋ'ʋlim, and Kenas begot and Kadmon begot

ref: the Hittites, the Perizzites, the Rephaites,
text: nɛ Hitti dim nɛ Perizi dim nɛ Refa dim
translated: and the Hittite people and the Perizzite people and the Rephaim people

ref: Abram's wife Sarai had not been able to have any children. But she owned a young Egyptian slave woman named Hagar,
text: Abram pu'a Sarai da nam pʋ du'a biiga. Amaa o da mɔr yammʋg onɛ an Egipt pu'asadir ka o yʋ'ʋr buon Haga.
translated: Abram’s wife Sarai had no children. But she had an Egyptian servant woman named Hagar.

ref: and asked, “Hagar, where have you come from, and where are you going?” She answered, “I'm running away from Sarai, my owner.”
text: Ka maliak la yɛl o ye, “Haga onɛ an Sarai yammʋga, fʋ yi yaanɛ na, ka wa'ae yaanɛ?” Ka o gandig ye, “M zɔtnɛ yiti m zugdaan Sarai san'an.”
translated: The angel of the Lord said to her, “Hagar, servant of Sarai, where have you come from, and where are you going?” She answered, “I am running away from my mistress Sarai.”

ref: But your son will live far from his relatives; he will be like a wild donkey, fighting everyone, and everyone fighting him.”
text: Biig la na gɔɔnd nwɛnɛ wɛog bʋŋ nɛ ka kisig sɔ' wʋsa, ka sɔ' wʋsa mɛ na kisig o, ka on nɛ o ba'abiis na an dataas nɛ taaba.”
translated: The child who refuses to listen to advice and insists on their own way, if their way fails, they will remember the advice of their elders and peers.

ref: I will bless her, and you will have a son by her. She will become the mother of nations, and some of her descendants will even be kings.
text: Man na niŋ o bareka ka o na du'a biribiŋi tisif. M mɛ na niŋ o bareka ka o lieb buudi bɛdegʋ yaab. Ka buudi na'anam na yii o san'an na.”
translated: He did not take his knife because he did not want to kill anyone. I also did not take his knife because I do not want to cause any trouble. And trouble is not good for anyone.

ref: But your son Isaac will be born about this time next year, and the promise I am making to you and your family will be for him and his descendants forever.
text: Amaa li anɛ fʋ biig Isaak onɛ ka Sara na du'a yʋʋm anwana win la na paam mam nɔnaar bareka la.”
translated: But when your child Isaac and Sarah were old, God still blessed them.

ref: The men turned and started toward Sodom. But the Lord stayed with Abraham.
text: Ka dapa ayi' la fɛndigi kpa' Sodom teŋin, ka Abraham nan kpɛn zi'e Zugsɔb la san'an.
translated: But he did not find even ten people in Sodom, and Abraham returned to his home.

ref: And Abraham asked him, “Lord, when you destroy the evil people, are you also going to destroy those who are good?
text: Ka Abraham li'el Zugsɔb la san'an ka bu'os o ye, “Fʋ na la'an san'am ninsʋma nɛ nimbɛ'ɛdnam la wʋsaa?
translated: And Abraham lifted up his eyes and saw, and behold, behind him was a ram caught by its horns in a thicket.

ref: Lot went outside and shut the door behind him.
text: Ka Lot yii ba san'an ka yɔ' za'anɔɔr la lɛbis
translated: But Lot’s wife looked back and became a pillar of salt

ref: There's a town near here. It's only a small place, but my family and I will be safe, if you let us go there.”
text: Gɔsima, tembili nwa' kpɛla yamma ka m na nyaŋi zɔɔ paae. Anina ka m na nyaŋi zɔɔ paae ka pɔns.”
translated: Yesterday, the child went to the market but I did not see him at all. Maybe I did not see him at all because of the crowd.

ref: and the Lord sent burning sulfur down like rain on Sodom and Gomorrah.
text: Ka Zugsɔb kɛ ka bugum nɛ kidibir yi saazugin sig Sodom nɛ Gomora zug nwɛnɛ saa niidnɛ.
translated: Just as fire and brimstone destroyed Sodom and Gomorrah in the olden days.

ref: He looked down toward Sodom and Gomorrah and saw smoke rising from all over the land—it was like a flaming furnace.
text: ka gɔs Sodom nɛ Gomora tɛɛns la baba wʋsa, ka nyɛ ka bugum nyɔ'ɔs duod teŋ la ni wʋʋ mɔɔg ka ba nyu'oe nɛ.
translated: When Sodom and Gomorrah were destroyed, fire and burning sulfur rained down from the sky and killed all the people there.

ref: But God came to Abimelech in a dream and said, “You have taken a married woman into your home, and for this you will die!”
text: Ka Wina'am keŋ Na'ab Abimelek san'an zaansʋŋin yɛl o ye, “Fʋ na kpi, bɔzugɔ pu'a kanɛ ka fʋ nɔk la mɔr sid!”
translated: But God came to King Abimelech in a dream at night and said to him, “You are about to die, because of the woman you have taken; she is a married woman!”

ref: Early the next morning Abimelech sent for his officials, and when he told them what had happened, they were frightened.
text: Bɛogʋ da nie la, Na'ab da isigi la'as o na'ayikpɛm ka yɛli ba on nyɛ si'el wʋsa, ka dabiem kpɛn' ba pamm.
translated: The chief is not at home, the chief's wife says he went to the farm early and has not yet returned.

ref: Abraham answered: I did it because I didn't think any of you respected God, and I was sure that someone would kill me to get my wife.
text: Ka Abraham lɛbis ye, “M tɛn'ɛs nɛ m sʋʋnrin sa, ye Wina'am zɔtim kae teŋgbauŋ kaŋa nwa' nii, alazug, ba na kʋʋmi m pu'a la zug.
translated: Then Abraham said, “I have dared to speak to the Lord, though I am nothing but dust and ashes, suppose the number of the righteous is five less than fifty, will you destroy the whole city because of five people?”

ref: and told Abraham he could settle anywhere in his country.
text: Ka Abimelek daa lɛn yɛl o ye, “Gɔsimi m sʋ'ʋlim la wʋsa gaŋ zin'ikanɛ ka fʋ bɔɔd ye fʋ zin'in.”
translated: But Abimelech also said to him, “See, my land is before you; live wherever you like.”

ref: and Sarah said, “God has made me laugh. And now everyone will laugh with me.
text: Ka Sara yɛl ye, “Wina'am kɛ ka m la', ka onɛ wʋm nɛ' mɛ na la'ad nɛ m.
translated: But Sara said, "Who would have told me that I would nurse children, yet I have given birth in his old age."

ref: and after they had run out of water, Hagar put her son under a bush.
text: Ka ku'om la da naae la ka o nɔk Isimael n digil tutuud ma'asim,
translated: But his mother did not want him to marry an Ishmaelite woman.

ref: Then she sat down a long way off, because she could not bear to watch him die. And she cried bitterly.
text: ka keŋ lalig bi'ela ka ye, “Mam kʋ nyaŋi gɔti m biig ka o kpiida!” Ka zin' lallisa ka kum.
translated: And he called the child and said, "Bring me a stick, child, and hit him!" And the child called out and died.

ref: When God heard the boy crying, the angel of God called out to Hagar from heaven and said, “Hagar, why are you worried? Don't be afraid. I have heard your son crying.
text: Wina'am daa wʋm biig la kaasig la, o maliak daa naam saazug ka buol Haga bu'os o ye, “Haga! Bɔɔ maalɛ? Da zɔt dabiem bɔzugɔ Wina'am wʋm biig la dig tutuud ma'asim kaasid la.
translated: Wina'am had a child but was poor, so his wife went to the market and asked Haga, “Haga! What should I do? There is nothing to feed Wina'am's child except for a little porridge.”

ref: Help him up and hold his hand, because I will make him the father of a great nation.”
text: Duom nɔk biig la; ka m na kɛ ka o buudi nɔbigi lieb bɛdegʋ.”
translated: Teach the child well; and when he grows up he will not forget what he has learned.

translated: But the chief priests plotted to kill him and Josef as well. They were afraid that the news would spread.

ref: “We each had a dream last night,” they answered, “and there is no one to tell us what they mean.” Joseph replied, “Doesn't God know the meaning of dreams? Now tell me what you dreamed.”
text: Ka ba lɛbis ye, “Ti babayi' nwa' sa zaansimnɛ yʋ'ʋŋ, amaa sɔ' kae na pa'ali ti li gbinnɛ.” Ka Josef lɛbis ye, “Abaŋ zaansʋŋ gbin pʋ yit nɛ Wina'am ni naa? Yɛliminimɛ ya zaansʋŋ la!”
translated: His brothers said, “We all had bundles of grain, but your bundle stood up and our bundles bowed down to it.” Then Joseph said, “Will you really rule over us or reign over us? They hated him even more because of his dreams!”

ref: and in three days the king will pardon you. He will make you his personal servant again, and you will serve him his wine, just as you used to do.
text: Daba atan' nwa' sʋʋgin, Faaro na yisif kpɛla ka kɛ ka fʋ lɛbi tʋmmi fʋ tʋʋma wʋʋ fʋn daa pʋn tʋm si'em la, ka fʋ na nɔk Faaro nwammi niŋ o nu'usin wʋʋ fʋn daa kuobid o daam si'em la.
translated: If you obey the law, Pharaoh will be pleased with you and will give you good things for your work every day, but if you disobey Pharaoh, he will punish you and take away your daily bread.

ref: I was kidnapped from the land of the Hebrews, and here in Egypt I haven't done anything to deserve being thrown in jail.
text: Ba mukim nɛ ziimɛ yi Hibiru teŋin na, hali kpɛla nwa' m pʋ tʋm si'el dinɛ nar ye m bɛ bʋmbɔk kaŋa nii.”
translated: They did not understand the words in the Hebrew language, so I had to explain everything to them in our own language.

ref: The top basket was full of all kinds of baked things for the king, but birds were eating them.”
text: ka pɛ'ɛkanɛ bɛ zugin la, ka bodobodo buudi wʋsa linɛ an Faaro din bɛɛ di ni, amaa ka niis ɔnbid bodobodo la.”
translated: If you put a stone in the sun, and a frog sits on it and says it is its own, it does not mean the stone belongs to the frog.

ref: and had the cook put to death. Everything happened just as Joseph had said it would,
text: ka yul onɛ aan o bodobodo maannib kpɛɛm la nwɛnɛ Josef n da pa'ali ba zaansʋŋ la gbin si'em la.
translated: But when his brothers saw that their father loved Joseph more than all his sons, they hated him and could not speak to him kindly.
